Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
refactor: remove HTML meta tag SEO blocking, rely on robots.txt only

Remove conflicting HTML meta tags that were blocking all search engines,
contradicting the robots.txt policy \(ADR-001\) that allows public content
indexing while protecting member data.

Changes:
- Remove <meta name="robots"> blocking tags from index.html
- Keep Open Graph tags for social media previews
- Update documentation to reflect robots.txt-only approach
- Add dev journal entry documenting policy simplification

Benefits:
- Single source of truth \(robots.txt\)
- Public content now indexable by search engines
- Member data remains protected
- Social media link previews work correctly
- Simpler architecture, no dynamic meta tag injection needed

Related: ADR-001 \(robots.txt policy decision\)

🤖 Generated with Claude Code \(https://claude.com/claude-code\)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
